dept_gba_corporation_desc: GBA (Greater Bengaluru Authority) is the apex statutory body governing Greater Bengaluru, established in 2025 under the Greater Bengaluru Governance Act 2024. It replaced BBMP and coordinates civic administration across 5 city corporations spanning 712 km², overseeing urban planning, infrastructure, and inter-agency coordination.
dept_gba_zone_desc: Zones are the first administrative subdivision within each GBA corporation, each managed by a Zonal Commissioner. Zones coordinate civic services, infrastructure projects, and municipal enforcement across a cluster of divisions and wards.
dept_gba_division_desc: Divisions are administrative subunits within GBA zones, coordinating ward-level infrastructure and service delivery across a smaller geographic area.
dept_gba_subdivision_desc: Subdivisions are the administrative tier below divisions, handling localised civic works, inspections, and coordination for a cluster of wards.
dept_gba_ward_desc: Wards are the basic unit of local representation in GBA. Each of the 368 wards across the 5 corporations elects one Corporator by direct vote for a 5-year term, responsible for advocating for local infrastructure and civic services.
dept_bbmp_zone_desc: BBMP (Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike) was Bengaluru's civic body from 2007 to 2025, handling roads, sanitation, property tax, health, and urban services. It was divided into 8 zones — Yelahanka, Dasarahalli, Rajarajeshwarinagara, Bommanahalli, South, West, East, and Mahadevapura — each managed by a Zonal Commissioner.
dept_bbmp_wards_desc: Ward boundaries used between 2023 and 2025 under BBMP, comprising 243 wards. Each ward elected one Corporator as the local representative. BBMP was dissolved and replaced by GBA and 5 city corporations in 2025.
dept_bbmp_wards_old_desc: Ward boundaries used before 2023 under BBMP, comprising 198 wards. This was the original delimitation when BBMP was constituted in 2007, before the expansion to 243 wards.
dept_bescom_zone_desc: BESCOM (Bangalore Electricity Supply Company) distributes electricity across Bengaluru and surrounding districts, serving over 8 million consumers. Zones are the top-level administrative grouping — BMAZ (North) covers the East and North circles, BMAZ (South) covers the South and West circles, BRAZ covers Bengaluru Rural areas, and CTAZ covers Tumkur and Davanagere circles.
dept_bescom_circle_desc: Circles are the primary operational units within each BESCOM zone, each headed by a Superintending Engineer. Circles coordinate capital works, fault management, and consumer services across a cluster of divisions.
dept_bescom_division_desc: BESCOM (Bangalore Electricity Supply Company) is the statutory body distributing electricity across Bengaluru and surrounding districts, serving over 8 million consumers. Divisions are the largest operational unit, each headed by a Divisional Engineer, overseeing capital works and maintenance across a cluster of subdivisions.
dept_bescom_subdivision_desc: Subdivisions are the primary operational unit within BESCOM, each headed by an Assistant Executive Engineer. They handle new service connections, fault rectification, meter reading oversight, and consumer billing for their area.
dept_bescom_section_desc: Sections are the frontline field unit of BESCOM, each headed by a Junior Engineer. They handle day-to-day line maintenance, outage response, meter inspections, and direct consumer service for a specific neighbourhood.
dept_bwssb_division_fullform: BWSSB (Bangalore Water Supply and Sewerage Board)
dept_bwssb_division_desc: BWSSB (Bangalore Water Supply and Sewerage Board), formed in 1964, is responsible for water supply and sewerage for Bengaluru. Divisions are the largest operational unit, each headed by an Executive Engineer, managing water distribution networks, sewerage systems, and consumer connections across a sector of the city.
dept_bwssb_subdivision_desc: Subdivisions are the intermediate operational unit within BWSSB, each under an Assistant Executive Engineer. They handle day-to-day maintenance, new connection approvals, and grievance resolution for a defined geographic area.
dept_bwssb_service_station_desc: Service stations are the most local BWSSB field unit, headed by a Junior Engineer. They directly handle consumer complaints, minor repairs, field inspections, and last-mile water and sewerage service for a specific neighbourhood.
dept_police_city_desc: Bengaluru City Police is responsible for law enforcement and public order in the city. The commissionerate has 11 law-and-order divisions, each headed by a Deputy Commissioner of Police, with police stations as the frontline units for crime registration, investigation, and community policing.
dept_police_traffic_dcp_desc: DCP (Deputy Commissioner of Police) Traffic zones are the top administrative tier of Bengaluru Traffic Police, with 4 zones — East, West, North, and South — each headed by a DCP. Each zone oversees several ACP sub-zones and all traffic police stations within its area.
dept_police_traffic_acp_desc: ACP (Assistant Commissioner of Police) Traffic zones are the intermediate administrative tier of Bengaluru Traffic Police, with 11 geographic zones each headed by an ACP. Each ACP zone oversees the traffic police stations within its area and reports to the respective DCP zone.
dept_police_traffic_desc: Bengaluru Traffic Police is a specialised wing of Bengaluru City Police responsible for managing road traffic, enforcing traffic rules, overseeing signal operations, and responding to accidents. It operates through 4 DCP zones, 11 ACP sub-zones, and over 45 traffic police stations across the city.
dept_bda_desc: BDA (Bangalore Development Authority) is the statutory planning authority for the Bengaluru Metropolitan Area, operating under the Karnataka Town and Country Planning Act 1961. It prepares the Master Plan, acquires land, develops residential layouts and infrastructure in areas beyond GBA's direct jurisdiction, and regulates land use in the wider metropolitan region.
dept_admin_district_desc: Revenue districts are headed by the Deputy Commissioner (DC), an IAS officer who is the principal administrative authority of the district. The DC oversees revenue collection, land records, law and order, disaster management, election administration, and coordination of state government schemes.
dept_admin_taluk_desc: Taluks are the primary subdivision of a district for revenue administration, each headed by a Tahsildar. The Tahsildar collects land revenue, maintains land records, issues income and caste certificates, processes land mutations, and resolves local revenue disputes.
dept_admin_hobli_desc: Hoblis are intermediate administrative units between the taluk and revenue village in Karnataka. Each hobli is a cluster of adjoining villages managed together for revenue administration, overseen by a Revenue Inspector who supervises village accountants.
dept_admin_village_desc: Revenue villages are the most fundamental administrative unit for land records and revenue collection. Each village has defined survey boundaries, a unique number, and land records maintained by a Village Accountant. The village is the basis for all property ownership records and agricultural statistics.
dept_election_pc_desc: Parliamentary constituencies (Lok Sabha) each directly elect one Member of Parliament (MP) by first-past-the-post vote. MPs serve 5-year terms, legislate at the national level, and represent approximately 1.5–2 million constituents in Parliament.
dept_election_ac_desc: Assembly constituencies (Vidhan Sabha) each directly elect one Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) to the Karnataka Vidhan Soudha. MLAs serve 5-year terms, legislate at the state level, and represent approximately 200,000–250,000 constituents.
dept_pincode_desc: Postal Index Numbers (PIN codes) are 6-digit codes introduced by India Post in 1972. The first 3 digits identify the sorting district; the last 3 identify the local post office. Pincode boundaries are defined by India Post based on delivery workload and geography.
dept_stamps_dro_desc: DRO (District Registrar Office) supervises all Sub-Registrar Offices within a revenue district. The District Registrar oversees property registration quality, resolves appeals against sub-registrar decisions, handles high-value registrations, and reconciles stamp duty collections across the district.
dept_stamps_sro_desc: SRO (Sub-Registrar Office) is the frontline office for property registration. Citizens register sale deeds, gift deeds, mortgages, leases, and other legal instruments here. The Sub-Registrar collects stamp duty and registration fees, verifies document compliance, and maintains the official register of property transactions.
